events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON output in answer. Need analyze image: 4-gas monitor readings O2 20.5 % vol, LEL 0%, H2S 1 ppm, CO 3 ppm. Need determine acceptable entry limits per OSHA 1910.146. Standard acceptable entry conditions: O2 19.5-23.5% (actually OSHA defines oxygen content between 19.5 and 23.5 percent). LEL <10% (flammable gas vapor or mist <10% LEL, unless <5% for specific hot work? Generally <10% acceptable). H2S permissible exposure limit: OSHA 8-hr TWA 10 ppm, ceiling 15 ppm, peak 50 ppm for 10 min if no other exposure. Acceptable entry condition for permit space: atmospheric concentration of toxic substance below PEL? OSHA 1910.146(b) acceptable entry conditions include "atmosphere contains no flammable gas, vapor, or mist in excess of 10 percent of its lower flammable limit" and "force-ventilated if needed" and "atmospheric concentration of any substance for which a PEL exists is not capable of being reached". H2S 1 ppm <10 ppm; CO 8-hr TWA PEL 50 ppm, ceiling? CO OSHA PEL 50 ppm TWA; 3 ppm < 50. So readings within acceptable limits. Entry can be authorized, provided all permit procedures. Decision pass? Since stage planned, reading from monitor at pre-entry. But we only have single point mid-depth; OSHA requires testing in areas that may be hazardous, stratified, top/middle/bottom. Image says probe lowered to mid-depth. Need identify reading outside acceptable limits: none. Confidence high. Risk low. Value could be 0? value for numeric reading or computed quantity. Since task asks read all four channels and determine; value maybe not specific single quantity. We can set value 0 or maybe leave as 0. Percent complete 0 because planned. Component conditions none. workable empty. sound_source, event_time, rate 0. order empty. references include OSHA 1910.146. Findings: all readings within acceptable entry limits. Actions: Verify stratified testing and complete permit. Rationale. Need output JSON. Ensure raw JSON no code fence.

No reading exceeds applicable limits, so atmospheric conditions do not prohibit entry, provided remaining permit controls are in place.",
